headline: Cataract Surgeon in Chennai
subheadline: Choosing the right surgeon matters as much as choosing the right time. Dr. Rajeswari (AIIMS, DNB, FAICO) performs every cataract evaluation and surgery personally.
metaDescription: Looking for an experienced cataract surgeon in Chennai? Dr. Rajeswari (AIIMS-trained) offers personal, stitchless cataract surgery in KK Nagar.

whyPatientsChoose.title: Surgeon-Led, Start to Finish
whyPatientsChoose.description: Your evaluation, surgery, and every follow-up are handled personally by Dr. Rajeswari -- not delegated to a rotating surgical team.

processSteps.title: Surgical Evaluation
processSteps.description: A detailed assessment of your cataract, eye health, and suitability for surgery -- performed by Dr. Rajeswari directly.

processSteps.title: A Direct Conversation
processSteps.description: Your surgeon explains the procedure, lens options, and realistic outcomes -- not a counselor or coordinator standing in.

processSteps.title: Stitchless Surgery
processSteps.description: Modern phacoemulsification technique, typically with same-day discharge.

landingFaqs.question: Will Dr. Rajeswari actually perform my surgery, or will it be handled by a surgical team?
landingFaqs.answer: Dr. Rajeswari performs the evaluation and the surgery personally -- there's no handoff to a different surgeon on the day of your procedure.

landingFaqs.question: What makes one cataract surgeon a better choice than another?
landingFaqs.answer: Experience with your specific case, a clear explanation of lens options suited to your lifestyle, and continuity of care through every follow-up. Dr. Rajeswari is AIIMS-trained with a Medical Retina Fellowship, bringing both surgical and broader ophthalmic expertise to each case.

landingFaqs.question: Can I speak with the surgeon directly before deciding, rather than just a coordinator?
landingFaqs.answer: Yes -- your consultation is with Dr. Rajeswari directly, so any questions about the procedure, risks, or lens choice are answered by the person actually performing your surgery.

decisionSupport.suitabilityTitle: What to Look For in a Cataract Surgeon
decisionSupport.suitabilityIntro: Surgical outcomes depend heavily on experience and judgment -- not just the technology used. Beyond technical skill, continuity matters: the surgeon who evaluates you should be the same one operating, and the same one seeing you afterward.
evaluationSteps: Detailed surgical assessment by the operating surgeon
Direct discussion of lens options and expected outcomes
Clear explanation of risks specific to your case
A recommendation based on your daily life, not a standard protocol

doctorsPerspectiveStructured.patientConcern: Patients researching cataract surgery often tell me they're less worried about the procedure itself and more concerned about who is actually doing it -- will it really be the surgeon they consulted with?
doctorsPerspectiveStructured.response: It will. I evaluate every patient myself, perform the surgery myself, and see you for follow-up myself. That continuity matters -- you shouldn't have to re-explain your case to a different person at every stage of surgical care.

learnMoreLink.text: Read our full guide to cataract surgery
